column1: The onboarding wizard the user completes, and exactly how each answer drives the workout generator. Read this first: it is the entry point to the whole workbook. Every 'feeds' reference points to another sheet in this file. Mappings are deterministic lookups in code, never inferred at generation time, so identical answers always produce identical programs.

column5: What the coach does with it

column3: (no input) 'A few quick questions and your coach will build training around you.'
column5: Nothing. Intro screen.
Stored for tone and greetings only.
Computes age and chooses the SCREENING PATHWAY. 18+ adult screen; 16-17 young-person path + guardian consent; under 16 blocked. Age is never defaulted.
Nutrition calorie accuracy only. No effect on training.
Nutrition only.
Nutrition, plus a sanity check on starting loads.
Nutrition only.

column3: Build muscle / Lose fat / Get stronger / Stay healthy
column5: Sets the canonical goal: Build muscle=Hypertrophy, Lose fat=Fat Loss, Get stronger=Strength, Stay healthy=General Fitness. Drives which split, how much volume, and every set/rep/rest prescription.
column6: Split Selector, Weekly Volume, Prescription Logic

column2: Anywhere you especially want to grow? (pick up to 2)
column5: Focal points. Adds 3-4 weekly sets to each chosen muscle (cap 20), taken from the lowest-priority muscle. Can unlock a Focus day on 5-day splits.

column3: Beginner (under ~6 months consistent training, or returning after a long break) / Intermediate (~6 months to 3 years, comfortable with the main lifts) / Advanced (2+ years consistent, confident technique, has run structured progression)
column5: Gates split choice, volume targets and which exercises are allowed (Advanced lifts are locked to Advanced users).
column6: Split Selector, Weekly Volume, Safety Rules

column2: (if Int/Adv) Have you followed a structured program before?
column5: If No: the first block runs one level more conservative (higher RIR, no Advanced lifts) even though the chosen level is stored. Eases them in, then ramps.

column2: Which days can you train? (gym / lifting days only — sport goes in the activity question)
column5: The actual gym weekdays, not a count and not counting outside sport. The count picks the split; the specific days let the scheduler place sessions and rest days. These days are editable later: a user can move or swap a day and the scheduler re-plans without losing progress (see Claude Code note CC01).
Sets the session budget: how many exercises and working sets fit. Short sessions drop optional slots first.
If Always/Usually: exercises that want a spotter (barbell bench, back squat) are either swapped or given a safe-setup cue. This is what makes the spotter rule work.
The coarse equipment tier. Filters out exercises the user cannot possibly do.

column2: (if not Full Gym) Got any of these?
column3: Barbell + plates / Squat rack / Pull-up bar / Bench or sturdy chair / Kettlebell / Resistance bands / Sliders or a towel
column5: Adds exact equipment tags on top of the tier. Barbell and squat rack are tickable extras, not assumed for Home Basics. 'Sliders or a towel' means discs or a folded towel under the feet for exercises like hamstring curls. One resistance band alone unlocks a large part of the library.

column3: None / Lower back / Neck / Shoulders / Elbows / Wrists / Hips / Knees / Ankles / Achilles or calf / Feet
column5: Each chip maps to a body REGION. Every exercise is tagged with the regions it loads (Stress Regions), so a region is enough to protect the user — the exact diagnosis is not needed. An Achilles flag maps to the ankle/calf region and pulls out jumping and high-impact conditioning. Free text is removed here; anything unusual or medical belongs in the safety check, which routes to clearance.

Scanned by the coach for health red-flags (cardiac, neurological, pregnancy, 'my doctor said...'). If any are found, it routes to the screening/clearance path (S05), it does not just store the note. Never a substitute for the safety check. See Coach AI Operating Rules and Screening Outcomes.

column2: Do you play sport / active outside the gym?
column3: None / Basketball / Football / Running / Tennis / Swimming / Cycling / Dance / Martial arts / Rock climbing / Gym classes / Walking or hiking / Rowing / Yoga or Pilates / Golf / Skating / Surfing / Active job / Other
column5: Counts as training the body already pays for. Cuts overlapping muscle volume, removes conditioning slots, keeps leg days away from leg-heavy sport days. Pulled into the workout generator (Claude Code note CC03). The whole step is optional and skippable, can be added later in-app (CC04).

column2: (if any) Which days, and how hard?
column5: The day drives scheduling adjacency; the intensity scales how much volume is cut and whether the next day runs easier.

column2: Any exercises you love? / Anything you'd rather never do?
column5: Favourites are weighted up when a slot allows; no-gos seed the exclusion list so they are never programmed. Optional; can be set later in-app.

column3: free text (placeholders: look better, summer body, get fitter, better routine)
column5: Tone and motivational framing only. No effect on the program structure.

column2: QUICK SAFETY CHECK  —  final step, gates whether any program is generated (Safety Rule S05)
1. Doctor ever said you have a heart condition, or to only exercise under supervision?
2. Do you feel chest pain during physical activity?
column5: Yes on 1-4, 6 or 7 -> REQUIRE PROFESSIONAL CLEARANCE: no program generates, user directed to a GP/physio, generation unlocks only after clearance. Chest pain at rest -> DO NOT GENERATE.

column2: 3. In the past month, chest pain when NOT active?
column5: Yes on 1-4, 6 or 7 -> REQUIRE PROFESSIONAL CLEARANCE: no program generates, user directed to a GP/physio, generation unlocks only after clearance. Chest pain at rest -> DO NOT GENERATE.

column2: 4. Do you lose balance from dizziness, or ever lose consciousness?
column5: Yes on 1-4, 6 or 7 -> REQUIRE PROFESSIONAL CLEARANCE: no program generates, user directed to a GP/physio, generation unlocks only after clearance. Chest pain at rest -> DO NOT GENERATE.

column2: 5. A bone, joint or soft-tissue problem that exercise could worsen?
column5: Yes -> triggers the joint follow-ups. Follow-ups decide: minor -> MODIFY_AND_CONTINUE with that region's rules; painful/treated/restricted/active -> professional clearance.

column2: 6. Currently pregnant, or given birth in the past 6 months?
column5: Yes on 1-4, 6 or 7 -> REQUIRE PROFESSIONAL CLEARANCE: no program generates, user directed to a GP/physio, generation unlocks only after clearance. Chest pain at rest -> DO NOT GENERATE.

column2: 7. Any other reason you shouldn't do physical activity?
column5: Yes on 1-4, 6 or 7 -> REQUIRE PROFESSIONAL CLEARANCE: no program generates, user directed to a GP/physio, generation unlocks only after clearance. Chest pain at rest -> DO NOT GENERATE.

column2: Follow-ups (only if Q5 Yes or an injury flagged): painful now? / under treatment? / exercise restricted? / resolved or active? / which movements hurt?
column5: Any of: painful now, under treatment, restricted, still active -> professional clearance, no program. All mild -> MODIFY_AND_CONTINUE, and 'which movements hurt' is stored as the affected region for exclusion rules.
Recorded with the screening result. Sits on top of the real screen, never instead of it.

column1: After onboarding, the generator runs the 14-step Generator Flow:

Screen -> pick split -> schedule around commitments -> budget weekly volume -> build each session from templates -> prescribe sets/reps/RIR -> set starting loads light -> then continuously: progress from logged performance, swap on request, adapt the calendar, deload, and re-screen. Every runtime change is re-checked against the Safety Rules. See the Generator Flow sheet.
